# The initial E2EE password generated by Rocket.Chat mobile can be recovered in a practical timescale.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Report ID** | [2546437](https://hackerone.com/reports/2546437) |
| **Program** | [Rocket.Chat](../by-program/rocket_chat.md) |
| **Severity** | High — CVSS 7.2 |
| **Weakness** | [Use of Insufficiently Random Values](../by-weakness/use-of-insufficiently-random-values.md) |
| **Bounty** | — |
| **Votes** | 23 |
| **Reporter** | [h0011](https://hackerone.com/h0011) |
| **Asset** | https://github.com/RocketChat/Rocket.Chat.ReactNative (SOURCE_CODE) |
| **CVE IDs** | CVE-2024-42027 |
| **Reported** | 2024-06-11 |
| **Disclosed** | 2024-10-01 |
| **Resolution** | Resolved |

## Program Summary

> The E2EE password entropy generated by Rocket.Chat Mobile prior to version 4.5.1 is insufficient, allowing attackers to crack it if they have the appropriate time and resources.

Special thanks to Hayato Kimura, Ryoma Ito, Kazuhiko Minematsu, and Takanori Isobe for their research.

## Vulnerability Details

*No vulnerability information available.*
